data.narrative:
Latitude is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised €50M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies: Aalyria ($1.3B), Stoke Space ($1.0B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here, given the availability of direct comparables at identical stage and sector. The median valuation across these four peers is $700M, with the range spanning $274M to $1.3B. A public-comp anchoring method using the 5-ticker Space & Aerospace public median (45.3x P/S) cannot be applied, as Latitude's revenue is not disclosed and private companies rarely make such figures available.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $595M to $805M, centered at $700M. The width of this range ($210M spread) reflects variance within the peer cohort rather than methodological diversification—only one quantitative method is available. Key uncertainties include the relative maturity and addressable market position of Latitude against its peers, neither of which can be assessed from valuation multiples alone. Revenue trajectory and unit economics would materially inform whether Latitude should sit toward the lower or upper end of the peer distribution.
